1172 tools, one-liners, and resources — personalized for Ali's projects

terminal: List of commands you use most often

List of commands you use most often

history | \
awk '{CMD[$2]++;count++;}END { for (a in CMD)print CMD[a] " " CMD[a]/count*100 "% " a;}' | \
grep -v "./" | \
column -c3 -s " " -t | \
sort -nr | nl |  head -n 20
Shell One-linersterminalone-liner

Shell command: List of commands you use most often

💡 Analyze Janus server logs for error patterns

Janus
terminal: Sterilize bash history

Sterilize bash history

function sterile() {

  history | awk '$2 != "history" { $1=""; print $0 }' | egrep -vi "\
curl\b+.*(-E|--cert)\b+.*\b*|\
curl\b+.*--pass\b+.*\b*|\
curl\b+.*(-U|--proxy-user).*:.*\b*|\
curl\b+.*(-u|--user).*:.*\b*
.*(-H|--header).*(token|auth.*)\b+.*|\
wget\b+.*--.*password\b+.*\b*|\
http.?://.+:.+@.*\
" > $HOME/histbuff; history -r $HOME/histbuff;

}

export PROMPT_COMMAND="sterile"
Shell One-linersterminalone-liner

Shell command: Sterilize bash history

💡 Kill zombie processes left by crashed Janus workers

Janus

Quickly backup a file

cp filename{,.orig}
Shell One-linersterminalone-liner

__EOF__

💡 Trace system calls when Janus video pipeline hangs

Janus
terminal: Run command(s) after exit session

Run command(s) after exit session

cat > /etc/profile << __EOF__
_after_logout() {

  username=$(whoami)

  for _pid in $(ps afx | grep sshd | grep "$username" | awk '{print $1}') ; do

    kill -9 $_pid

  done

}
trap _after_logout EXIT
__EOF__
Shell One-linersterminalone-liner

Shell command: Run command(s) after exit session

💡 Monitor Janus Docker container resource usage in real-time

JanusPhynxTimer
terminal: Generate a sequence of numbers

Generate a sequence of numbers

for ((i=1; i<=10; i+=2)) ; do echo $i ; done
# alternative: seq 1 2 10

for ((i=5; i<=10; ++i)) ; do printf '%02d\n' $i ; done
# alternative: seq -w 5 10

for i in {1..10} ; do echo $i ; done
Shell One-linersterminalone-liner

Shell command: Generate a sequence of numbers

lsof: Lists all listening ports together with the PID of the associated process

Lists all listening ports together with the PID of the associated process

lsof -Pan -i tcp -i udp
Shell One-linerslsofone-liner

Shell command: Lists all listening ports together with the PID of the associated process

💡 Could help diagnose problems in your Janus stack

Janus
lsof: List all open ports and their owning executables

List all open ports and their owning executables

lsof -i -P | grep -i "listen"
Shell One-linerslsofone-liner

Shell command: List all open ports and their owning executables

💡 Relevant to the technologies used in Janus

JanusPhynxTimer
lsof: Show all open ports

Show all open ports

lsof -Pnl -i
Shell One-linerslsofone-liner

Shell command: Show all open ports

💡 Debug why your Janus Flask server won't restart on port 5000

Janus
lsof: Show open ports (LISTEN)

Show open ports (LISTEN)

lsof -Pni4 | grep LISTEN | column -t
Shell One-linerslsofone-liner

Shell command: Show open ports (LISTEN)

💡 Profile Janus Python processes eating too much CPU during detection

Janus
lsof: List all files opened by a particular command

List all files opened by a particular command

lsof -c "process"
Shell One-linerslsofone-liner

Shell command: List all files opened by a particular command

💡 Trace system calls when Janus video pipeline hangs

Janus
lsof: View user activity per directory

View user activity per directory

lsof -u username -a +D /etc
Shell One-linerslsofone-liner

Shell command: View user activity per directory

💡 Monitor Janus Docker container resource usage in real-time

Janus
lsof: Show 10 largest open files

Show 10 largest open files

lsof / | \
awk '{ if($7 > 1048576) print $7/1048576 "MB" " " $9 " " $1 }' | \
sort -n -u | tail | column -t
Shell One-linerslsofone-liner

Shell command: Show 10 largest open files

💡 Find which process is holding Janus's SQLite database lock

Janus

Show current working directory of a process

lsof -p <PID> | grep cwd
Shell One-linerslsofone-liner

Shell command: Show current working directory of a process

find (15)

find: Find files that have been modified on your system in the past 60 minutes

Find files that have been modified on your system in the past 60 minutes

find / -mmin 60 -type f

Shell command: Find files that have been modified on your system in the past 60 minutes

Janus
find: Find all files larger than 20M

Find all files larger than 20M

find / -type f -size +20M
Shell One-linersfindone-liner

Shell command: Find all files larger than 20M

💡 Kill zombie processes left by crashed Janus workers

Janus
find: Find duplicate files (based on MD5 hash)

Find duplicate files (based on MD5 hash)

find -type f -exec md5sum '{}' ';' | sort | uniq --all-repeated=separate -w 33
Shell One-linersfindone-liner

Shell command: Find duplicate files (based on MD5 hash)

💡 Test Janus API endpoints with automated HTTP requests

JanusPhynxTimer
find: Change permission only for files

Change permission only for files

cd /var/www/site && find . -type f -exec chmod 766 {} \;
cd /var/www/site && find . -type f -exec chmod 664 {} +
Shell One-linersfindone-liner

Shell command: Change permission only for files

💡 Useful for Janus development and debugging workflows

Janus
find: Change permission only for directories

Change permission only for directories

cd /var/www/site && find . -type d -exec chmod g+x {} \;
cd /var/www/site && find . -type d -exec chmod g+rwx {} +
Shell One-linersfindone-liner

Shell command: Change permission only for directories

💡 Add to your Janus development toolkit

Janus
find: Find files and directories for specific user/group

Find files and directories for specific user/group

# User:
find . -user <username> -print
find /etc -type f -user <username> -name "*.conf"

# Group:
find /opt -group <group>
find /etc -type f -group <group> -iname "*.conf"
Shell One-linersfindone-liner

Shell command: Find files and directories for specific user/group

💡 Reference when troubleshooting Janus issues

Janus
find: Find files and directories for all without specific user/group

Find files and directories for all without specific user/group

# User:
find . \! -user <username> -print

# Group:
find . \! -group <group>
Shell One-linersfindone-liner

Shell command: Find files and directories for all without specific user/group

💡 Could help diagnose problems in your Janus stack

Janus
find: Looking for files/directories that only have certain permission

Looking for files/directories that only have certain permission

# User
find . -user <username> -perm -u+rw # -rw-r--r--
find /home -user $(whoami) -perm 777 # -rwxrwxrwx

# Group:
find /home -type d -group <group> -perm 755 # -rwxr-xr-x
Shell One-linersfindone-liner

Shell command: Looking for files/directories that only have certain permission

💡 Relevant to the technologies used in Janus

Janus
find: Delete older files than 60 days

Delete older files than 60 days

find . -type f -mtime +60 -delete
Shell One-linersfindone-liner

Shell command: Delete older files than 60 days

Janus

Recursively remove all empty sub-directories from a directory

find . -depth  -type d  -empty -exec rmdir {} \;
Shell One-linersfindone-liner

Shell command: Recursively remove all empty sub-directories from a directory

Janus

How to find all hard links to a file

Janus
find: Recursively find the latest modified files

Recursively find the latest modified files

find . -type f -exec stat --format '%Y :%y %n' "{}" \; | sort -nr | cut -d: -f2- | head

Janus

Recursively find/replace of a string with sed

Recursively find/replace of a string in directories and file names

Shell command: Recursively find/replace of a string in directories and file names

strace (6)

strace: Track with child processes

Track with child processes

# 1)
strace -f -p $(pidof glusterfsd)

# 2)
strace -f $(pidof php-fpm | sed 's/\([0-9]*\)/\-p \1/g')
Shell One-linersstraceone-liner

Shell command: Track with child processes

💡 Debug why your Janus Flask server won't restart on port 5000

Janus
strace: Track process with 30 seconds limit

Track process with 30 seconds limit

timeout 30 strace $(< /var/run/zabbix/zabbix_agentd.pid)
Shell One-linersstraceone-liner

Shell command: Track process with 30 seconds limit

💡 Profile Janus Python processes eating too much CPU during detection

Janus
strace: Track processes and redirect output to a file

Track processes and redirect output to a file

ps auxw | grep '[a]pache' | awk '{print " -p " $2}' | \
xargs strace -o /tmp/strace-apache-proc.out
Shell One-linersstraceone-liner

Shell command: Track processes and redirect output to a file

💡 Trace system calls when Janus video pipeline hangs

JanusMoonlight
strace: Track with print time spent in each syscall and limit length of print strings

Track with print time spent in each syscall and limit length of print strings

ps auxw | grep '[i]init_policy' | awk '{print " -p " $2}' | \
xargs strace -f -e trace=network -T -s 10000
Shell One-linersstraceone-liner

Shell command: Track with print time spent in each syscall and limit length of print strings

Janus

Shell command: Track the open request of a network port

vimdiff: Compare Hex dump

tar (2)

tar: System backup with exclude specific directories

System backup with exclude specific directories

cd /
tar -czvpf /mnt/system$(date +%d%m%Y%s).tgz --directory=/ \
--exclude=proc/* --exclude=sys/* --exclude=dev/* --exclude=mnt/* .
Shell One-linerstarone-liner

Shell command: System backup with exclude specific directories

💡 Could help diagnose problems in your Janus stack

Janus
tar: System backup with exclude specific directories (pigz)

System backup with exclude specific directories (pigz)

cd /
tar cvpf /backup/snapshot-$(date +%d%m%Y%s).tgz --directory=/ \
--exclude=proc/* --exclude=sys/* --exclude=dev/* \
--exclude=mnt/* --exclude=tmp/* --use-compress-program=pigz .
Shell One-linerstarone-liner

Shell command: System backup with exclude specific directories (pigz)

💡 Relevant to the technologies used in Janus

Janus

dump (2)

dump: System backup to file

last (1)

last: Was the last reboot a panic?

Was the last reboot a panic?

(last -x -f $(ls -1t /var/log/wtmp* | head -2 | tail -1); last -x -f /var/log/wtmp) | \
grep -A1 reboot | head -2 | grep -q shutdown && echo "Expected reboot" || echo "Panic reboot"

Shell command: Was the last reboot a panic?

script (1)

script: Record and replay terminal session

Record and replay terminal session

### Record session
# 1)
script -t 2>~/session.time -a ~/session.log

# 2)
script --timing=session.time session.log

### Replay session
scriptreplay --timing=session.time session.log
Shell One-linersscriptone-liner

Shell command: Record and replay terminal session

💡 Add to your Janus development toolkit

JanusPhynxTimerRobinhood

du (1)

du: Show 20 biggest directories with 'K M G'

Show 20 biggest directories with 'K M G'

du | \
sort -r -n | \
awk '{split("K M G",v); s=1; while($1>1024){$1/=1024; s++} print int($1)" "v[s]"\t"$2}' | \
head -n 20
Shell One-linersduone-liner

Shell command: Show 20 biggest directories with 'K M G'

💡 Reference when troubleshooting Janus issues

Janus

inotifywait (1)

inotifywait: Init tool everytime a file in a directory is modified

Init tool everytime a file in a directory is modified

JanusMoonlight

openssl: Testing connection to the remote host with specific ssl version

Testing connection to the remote host with specific ssl version

openssl s_client -tls1_2 -connect google.com:443
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Testing connection to the remote host with specific ssl version

💡 Audit your Moonlight site headers for security best practices

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Testing connection to the remote host with specific ssl cipher

Testing connection to the remote host with specific ssl cipher

openssl s_client -cipher 'AES128-SHA' -connect google.com:443
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Testing connection to the remote host with specific ssl cipher

💡 Debug DNS propagation issues after Vercel domain changes

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Verify 0-RTT

Verify 0-RTT

_host="example.com"

cat > req.in << __EOF__
HEAD / HTTP/1.1
Host: $_host
Connection: close
__EOF__

openssl s_client -connect ${_host}:443 -tls1_3 -sess_out session.pem -ign_eof < req.in
openssl s_client -connect ${_host}:443 -tls1_3 -sess_in session.pem -early_data req.in
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Verify 0-RTT

💡 Profile Moonlight page load times and find bottlenecks

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate private key without passphrase

Generate private key without passphrase

# _len: 2048, 4096
( _fd="private.key" ; _len="2048" ; \
openssl genrsa -out ${_fd} ${_len} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate private key without passphrase

💡 Test your Moonlight CDN and caching configuration

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate private key with passphrase

Generate private key with passphrase

# _ciph: aes128, aes256
# _len: 2048, 4096
( _ciph="aes128" ; _fd="private.key" ; _len="2048" ; \
openssl genrsa -${_ciph} -out ${_fd} ${_len} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate private key with passphrase

💡 Scan moonlightanalytica.com for common web vulnerabilities

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Remove passphrase from private key

Remove passphrase from private key

( _fd="private.key" ; _fd_unp="private_unp.key" ; \
openssl rsa -in ${_fd} -out ${_fd_unp} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Remove passphrase from private key

💡 Verify HTTPS redirect chains on your Moonlight deployment

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Encrypt existing private key with a passphrase

Encrypt existing private key with a passphrase

# _ciph: aes128, aes256
( _ciph="aes128" ; _fd="private.key" ; _fd_pass="private_pass.key" ; \
openssl rsa -${_ciph} -in ${_fd} -out ${_fd_pass}
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Encrypt existing private key with a passphrase

💡 Check Moonlight's Content-Security-Policy headers

MoonlightJanusPhynxTimer
openssl: Check private key

Check private key

( _fd="private.key" ; \
openssl rsa -check -in ${_fd} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Check private key

💡 Benchmark your Moonlight site against competitor load times

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Get public key from private key

Get public key from private key

( _fd="private.key" ; _fd_pub="public.key" ; \
openssl rsa -pubout -in ${_fd} -out ${_fd_pub} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Get public key from private key

💡 Monitor Moonlight uptime and response codes

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate private key and CSR

Generate private key and CSR

( _fd="private.key" ; _fd_csr="request.csr" ; _len="2048" ; \
openssl req -out ${_fd_csr} -new -newkey rsa:${_len} -nodes -keyout ${_fd} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate private key and CSR

💡 Optimize Moonlight CSS delivery and render-blocking resources

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate CSR

Generate CSR

( _fd="private.key" ; _fd_csr="request.csr" ; \
openssl req -out ${_fd_csr} -new -key ${_fd} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate CSR

💡 Test Moonlight's mobile responsiveness from the command line

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate CSR (metadata from existing certificate)

Generate CSR (metadata from existing certificate)

( _fd="private.key" ; _fd_csr="request.csr" ; _fd_crt="cert.crt" ; \
openssl x509 -x509toreq -in ${_fd_crt} -out ${_fd_csr} -signkey ${_fd} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate CSR (metadata from existing certificate)

💡 Useful for Moonlight development and debugging workflows

MoonlightJanusCaposeo
openssl: Generate CSR with -config param

Generate CSR with -config param

( _fd="private.key" ; _fd_csr="request.csr" ; \
openssl req -new -sha256 -key ${_fd} -out ${_fd_csr} \
-config <(
cat << __EOF__
[req]
default_bits        = 2048
default_md          = sha256
prompt              = no
distinguished_name  = dn
req_extensions      = req_ext

[ dn ]
C   = "<two-letter ISO abbreviation for your country>"
ST  = "<state or province where your organisation is legally located>"
L   = "<city where your organisation is legally located>"
O   = "<legal name of your organisation>"
OU  = "<section of the organisation>"
CN  = "<fully qualified domain name>"

[ req_ext ]
subjectAltName = @alt_names

[ alt_names ]
DNS.1 = <fully qualified domain name>
DNS.2 = <next domain>
DNS.3 = <next domain>
__EOF__
))
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate CSR with -config param

💡 Add to your Moonlight development toolkit

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate CSR with -config param

Generate CSR with -config param

countryName            = "DE"                     # C=
stateOrProvinceName    = "Hessen"                 # ST=
localityName           = "Keller"                 # L=
postalCode             = "424242"                 # L/postalcode=
postalAddress          = "Keller"                 # L/postaladdress=
streetAddress          = "Crater 1621"            # L/street=
organizationName       = "apfelboymschule"        # O=
organizationalUnitName = "IT Department"          # OU=
commonName             = "example.com"            # CN=
emailAddress           = "webmaster@example.com"  # CN/emailAddress=
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate CSR with -config param

💡 Reference when troubleshooting Moonlight issues

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate CSR with -config param

Generate CSR with -config param

[req]
...
oid_section         = new_oids

[ new_oids ]
postalCode = 2.5.4.17
streetAddress = 2.5.4.9
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate CSR with -config param

💡 Could help diagnose problems in your Moonlight stack

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate CSR with -config param

Generate CSR with -config param

( _fd="private.key" ; _fd_csr="request.csr" ; \
openssl req -new -sha256 -key ${_fd} -out ${_fd_csr} \
-config <(
cat << __EOF__
[req]
default_bits        = 2048
default_md          = sha256
prompt              = no
distinguished_name  = dn
req_extensions      = req_ext
oid_section         = new_oids

[ new_oids ]
serialNumber = 2.5.4.5
streetAddress = 2.5.4.9
postalCode = 2.5.4.17
businessCategory = 2.5.4.15

[ dn ]
serialNumber=00001111
businessCategory=Private Organization
jurisdictionC=DE
C=DE
ST=Hessen
L=Keller
postalCode=424242
streetAddress=Crater 1621
O=AV Company
OU=IT
CN=example.com

[ req_ext ]
subjectAltName = @alt_names

[ alt_names ]
DNS.1 = example.com
__EOF__
))
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate CSR with -config param

MoonlightJanus
openssl: List available EC curves

List available EC curves

openssl ecparam -list_curves
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: List available EC curves

💡 Use to check SSL/TLS config on moonlightanalytica.com before deploy

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Print ECDSA private and public keys

Print ECDSA private and public keys

( _fd="private.key" ; \
openssl ec -in ${_fd} -noout -text )

# For x25519 only extracting public key
( _fd="private.key" ; _fd_pub="public.key" ; \
openssl pkey -in ${_fd} -pubout -out ${_fd_pub} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Print ECDSA private and public keys

💡 Audit your Moonlight site headers for security best practices

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate ECDSA private key

Generate ECDSA private key

# _curve: prime256v1, secp521r1, secp384r1
( _fd="private.key" ; _curve="prime256v1" ; \
openssl ecparam -out ${_fd} -name ${_curve} -genkey )

# _curve: X25519
( _fd="private.key" ; _curve="x25519" ; \
openssl genpkey -algorithm ${_curve} -out ${_fd} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate ECDSA private key

💡 Debug DNS propagation issues after Vercel domain changes

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate private key and CSR (ECC)

Generate private key and CSR (ECC)

# _curve: prime256v1, secp521r1, secp384r1
( _fd="domain.com.key" ; _fd_csr="domain.com.csr" ; _curve="prime256v1" ; \
openssl ecparam -out ${_fd} -name ${_curve} -genkey ; \
openssl req -new -key ${_fd} -out ${_fd_csr} -sha256 )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate private key and CSR (ECC)

💡 Profile Moonlight page load times and find bottlenecks

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate self-signed certificate

Generate self-signed certificate

# _len: 2048, 4096
( _fd="domain.key" ; _fd_out="domain.crt" ; _len="2048" ; _days="365" ; \
openssl req -newkey rsa:${_len} -nodes \
-keyout ${_fd} -x509 -days ${_days} -out ${_fd_out} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate self-signed certificate

💡 Test your Moonlight CDN and caching configuration

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate self-signed certificate from existing private key

Generate self-signed certificate from existing private key

# _len: 2048, 4096
( _fd="domain.key" ; _fd_out="domain.crt" ; _days="365" ; \
openssl req -key ${_fd} -nodes \
-x509 -days ${_days} -out ${_fd_out} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate self-signed certificate from existing private key

💡 Scan moonlightanalytica.com for common web vulnerabilities

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Generate self-signed certificate from existing private key and csr

Generate self-signed certificate from existing private key and csr

# _len: 2048, 4096
( _fd="domain.key" ; _fd_csr="domain.csr" ; _fd_out="domain.crt" ; _days="365" ; \
openssl x509 -signkey ${_fd} -nodes \
-in ${_fd_csr} -req -days ${_days} -out ${_fd_out} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Generate self-signed certificate from existing private key and csr

openssl pkcs12 -in ${_fd_pfx} -nodes -out ${_fd_pem} )
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Extract private key and certs from pfx

💡 Optimize Moonlight CSS delivery and render-blocking resources

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Extract certs from p7b

Extract certs from p7b

# PKCS#7 file doesn't include private keys.
( _fd_p7b="cert.p7b" ; _fd_pem="cert.pem" ; \
openssl pkcs7 -inform DER -outform PEM -in ${_fd_p7b} -print_certs > ${_fd_pem})
# or:
openssl pkcs7 -print_certs -in -in ${_fd_p7b} -out ${_fd_pem})
Shell One-linersopensslone-liner

Shell command: Extract certs from p7b

💡 Test Moonlight's mobile responsiveness from the command line

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Convert DER to PEM

Convert DER to PEM

( _fd_der="cert.crt" ; _fd_pem="cert.pem" ; \
openssl x509 -in ${_fd_der} -inform der -outform pem -out ${_fd_pem} )

openssl x509 -in ${_fd_pem} -outform der -out ${_fd_der} )

MoonlightJanus
openssl: Verification of the public key

Verification of the public key

# 1)
( _fd="public.key" ; \
openssl pkey -noout -text -pubin -in ${_fd} )

# 2)
( _fd="private.key" ; \
openssl rsa -inform PEM -noout -in ${_fd} &> /dev/null ; \
if [ $? = 0 ] ; then echo -en "OK\n" ; fi )

curl: Repeat URL request

Repeat URL request

# URL sequence substitution with a dummy query string:
curl -ks https://example.com/?[1-20]

# With shell 'for' loop:
for i in {1..20} ; do curl -ks https://example.com/ ; done
Shell One-linerscurlone-liner

Shell command: Repeat URL request

💡 Reference when troubleshooting Robinhood issues

RobinhoodJanus
curl: Check DNS and HTTP trace with headers for specific domains

Check DNS and HTTP trace with headers for specific domains

### Set domains and external dns servers.
_domain_list=(google.com) ; _dns_list=("8.8.8.8" "1.1.1.1")

for _domain in "${_domain_list[@]}" ; do

  printf '=%.0s' {1..48}

  echo

  printf "[\\e[1;32m+\\e[m] resolve: %s\\n" "$_domain"

  for _dns in "${_dns_list[@]}" ; do

    # Resolve domain.
    host "${_domain}" "${_dns}"

    echo

  done

  for _proto in http https ; do

    printf "[\\e[1;32m+\\e[m] trace + headers: %s://%s\\n" "$_proto" "$_domain"

    # Get trace and http headers.
    curl -Iks -A "x-agent" --location "${_proto}://${_domain}"

    echo

  done

done

unset _domain_list _dns_list
Shell One-linerscurlone-liner

Shell command: Check DNS and HTTP trace with headers for specific domains

💡 Test your Moonlight CDN and caching configuration

MoonlightJanusCaposeo

ssh (14)

ssh: Escape Sequence

Escape Sequence

# Supported escape sequences:
~.  - terminate connection (and any multiplexed sessions)
~B  - send a BREAK to the remote system
~C  - open a command line
~R  - Request rekey (SSH protocol 2 only)
~^Z - suspend ssh
~#  - list forwarded connections
~&  - background ssh (when waiting for connections to terminate)
~?  - this message
~~  - send the escape character by typing it twice
Shell One-linerssshone-liner

Shell command: Escape Sequence

💡 Relevant to the technologies used in Janus

Janus

ssh: Simple recording SSH session

Simple recording SSH session

function _ssh_sesslog() {

  _sesdir="<path/to/session/logs>"

  mkdir -p "${_sesdir}" && \
  ssh $@ 2>&1 | tee -a "${_sesdir}/$(date +%Y%m%d).log"

}

# Alias:
alias ssh='_ssh_sesslog'
Shell One-linerssshone-liner

Shell command: Simple recording SSH session

💡 Debug Janus API response times under load

JanusPhynxTimer
ssh: Using Keychain for SSH logins

Using Keychain for SSH logins

### Delete all of ssh-agent's keys.
function _scl() {

  /usr/bin/keychain --clear

}

### Add key to keychain.
function _scg() {

  /usr/bin/keychain /path/to/private-key
  source "$HOME/.keychain/$HOSTNAME-sh"

}
Shell One-linerssshone-liner

Shell command: Using Keychain for SSH logins

# Forwarding our local 2250 port to nmap.org:443 from localhost through localhost

# Connect to the service:
host1> curl -Iks --location -X GET https://localhost:2250

Janus
ssh: SSH local port forwarding

SSH local port forwarding

# Forwarding our local 9051 port to db.d.x:5432 from localhost through node.d.y
host1> ssh -nNT -L 9051:db.d.x:5432 node.d.y

# Connect to the service:
host1> psql -U db_user -d db_dev -p 9051 -h localhost
Shell One-linerssshone-liner

Shell command: SSH local port forwarding

Janus
ssh: SSH remote port forwarding

SSH remote port forwarding

# Forwarding our local 9051 port to db.d.x:5432 from host2 through node.d.y
host1> ssh -nNT -R 9051:db.d.x:5432 node.d.y

# Connect to the service:
host2> psql -U postgres -d postgres -p 8000 -h localhost
Shell One-linerssshone-liner

Shell command: SSH remote port forwarding

'pass=|pwd=|log=|login=|user=|username=|pw=|passw=|passwd=|password=|pass:|user:|username:|password:|login:|pass |user ' \
--color=auto --line-buffered -B20
Shell One-linerstcpdumpone-liner

Shell command: Grab user and pass ever plain http

💡 Monitor Janus Docker container resource usage in real-time

JanusMoonlight

Shell One-linersnmapone-liner

Shell command: Nmap scan and pass output to Nikto

💡 Add to your Janus development toolkit

Janus
nmap: Recon specific ip:service with Nmap NSE scripts stack

Recon specific ip:service with Nmap NSE scripts stack

# Set variables:
_hosts="192.168.250.10"
_ports="80,443"

# Set Nmap NSE scripts stack:
_nmap_nse_scripts="+dns-brute,\
                   +http-auth-finder,\
                   +http-chrono,\
                   +http-cookie-flags,\
                   +http-cors,\
                   +http-cross-domain-policy,\
                   +http-csrf,\
                   +http-dombased-xss,\
                   +http-enum,\
                   +http-errors,\
                   +http-git,\
                   +http-grep,\
                   +http-internal-ip-disclosure,\
                   +http-jsonp-detection,\
                   +http-malware-host,\
                   +http-methods,\
                   +http-passwd,\
                   +http-phpself-xss,\
                   +http-php-version,\
                   +http-robots.txt,\
                   +http-sitemap-generator,\
                   +http-shellshock,\
                   +http-stored-xss,\
                   +http-title,\
                   +http-unsafe-output-escaping,\
                   +http-useragent-tester,\
                   +http-vhosts,\
                   +http-waf-detect,\
                   +http-waf-fingerprint,\
                   +http-xssed,\
                   +traceroute-geolocation.nse,\
                   +ssl-enum-ciphers,\
                   +whois-domain,\
                   +whois-ip"

# Set Nmap NSE script params:
_nmap_nse_scripts_args="dns-brute.domain=${_hosts},http-cross-domain-policy.domain-lookup=true,"
_nmap_nse_scripts_args+="http-waf-detect.aggro,http-waf-detect.detectBodyChanges,"
_nmap_nse_scripts_args+="http-waf-fingerprint.intensive=1"

# Perform scan:
nmap --script="$_nmap_nse_scripts" --script-args="$_nmap_nse_scripts_args" -p "$_ports" "$_hosts"
Shell One-linersnmapone-liner

Shell command: Recon specific ip:service with Nmap NSE scripts stack

💡 Reference when troubleshooting Janus issues

JanusRobinhood

Launch remote shell

# 1)
server> nc -l 5000 -e /bin/bash
client> nc 10.240.30.3 5000

# 2)
server> rm -f /tmp/f; mkfifo /tmp/f
server> cat /tmp/f | /bin/bash -i 2>&1 | nc -l 127.0.0.1 5000 > /tmp/f
client> nc 10.240.30.3 5000

netcat: Simple HTTP Server

Simple HTTP Server

cat > index.html << __EOF__
<!doctype html>
    <head>
        <meta charset="utf-8">
        <meta http-equiv="X-UA-Compatible" content="IE=edge,chrome=1">
        <title></title>
        <meta name="description" content="">
        <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
    </head>
    <body>

    <p>

      Hello! It's a site.

    </p>

    </body>
</html>
__EOF__

netcat: Simple HTTP Server

Simple HTTP Server

server> while : ; do \
(echo -ne "HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\nContent-Length: $(wc -c <index.html)\r\n\r\n" ; cat index.html;) | \
nc -l -p 5000 \
; done
Shell One-linersnetcatone-liner

Shell command: Simple HTTP Server

💡 Monitor Janus Docker container resource usage in real-time

JanusMoonlight
netcat: Simple HTTP Proxy (single connection)

Simple HTTP Proxy (single connection)

#!/usr/bin/env bash

if [[ $# != 2 ]] ; then
  printf "%s\\n" \
         "usage: ./nc-proxy listen-port bk_host:bk_port"
fi

_listen_port="$1"
_bk_host=$(echo "$2" | cut -d ":" -f1)
_bk_port=$(echo "$2" | cut -d ":" -f2)

printf "  lport: %s\\nbk_host: %s\\nbk_port: %s\\n\\n" \
       "$_listen_port" "$_bk_host" "$_bk_port"

_tmp=$(mktemp -d)
_back="$_tmp/pipe.back"
_sent="$_tmp/pipe.sent"
_recv="$_tmp/pipe.recv"

trap 'rm -rf "$_tmp"' EXIT

mkfifo -m 0600 "$_back" "$_sent" "$_recv"

sed "s/^/=> /" <"$_sent" &
sed "s/^/<=  /" <"$_recv" &

nc -l -p "$_listen_port" <"$_back" | \
tee "$_sent" | \
nc "$_bk_host" "$_bk_port" | \
tee "$_recv" >"$_back"
Shell One-linersnetcatone-liner

Shell command: Simple HTTP Proxy (single connection)

💡 Verify HTTPS redirect chains on your Moonlight deployment

MoonlightJanus
netcat: Simple HTTP Proxy (single connection)

Simple HTTP Proxy (single connection)

server> chmod +x nc-proxy && ./nc-proxy 8080 192.168.252.10:8000
  lport: 8080
bk_host: 192.168.252.10
bk_port: 8000

client> http -p h 10.240.30.3:8080
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Accept-Ranges: bytes
Cache-Control: max-age=31536000
Content-Length: 2748
Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
Date: Sun, 01 Jul 2018 20:12:08 GMT
Last-Modified: Sun, 01 Apr 2018 21:53:37 GMT
Shell One-linersnetcatone-liner

Shell command: Simple HTTP Proxy (single connection)

💡 Check Moonlight's Content-Security-Policy headers

MoonlightJanus
netcat: Create a single-use TCP or UDP proxy

Create a single-use TCP or UDP proxy

### TCP -> TCP
nc -l -p 2000 -c "nc [ip|hostname] 3000"

### TCP -> UDP
nc -l -p 2000 -c "nc -u [ip|hostname] 3000"

### UDP -> UDP
nc -l -u -p 2000 -c "nc -u [ip|hostname] 3000"

### UDP -> TCP
nc -l -u -p 2000 -c "nc [ip|hostname] 3000"
Shell One-linersnetcatone-liner

Shell command: Create a single-use TCP or UDP proxy

💡 Find which process is holding Janus's SQLite database lock

Janus

netstat: Graph # of connections for each hosts

Graph # of connections for each hosts

sort | uniq -c | awk '{ printf("%s\t%s\t",$2,$1) ; for (i = 0; i < $1; i++) {printf("*")}; print "" }'

network-other (2)

network-other: Get all subnets for specific AS (Autonomous system)

Get all subnets for specific AS (Autonomous system)

AS="AS32934"
whois -h whois.radb.net -- "-i origin ${AS}" | \
grep "^route:" | \
cut -d ":" -f2 | \
sed -e 's/^[ \t]//' | \
sort -n -t . -k 1,1 -k 2,2 -k 3,3 -k 4,4 | \
cut -d ":" -f2 | \
sed -e 's/^[ \t]/allow /' | \
sed 's/$/;/' | \
sed 's/allow  */subnet -> /g'
Shell One-linersnetwork-otherone-liner

Shell command: Get all subnets for specific AS (Autonomous system)

💡 Could help diagnose problems in your Janus stack

Janus

python (4)

python: Static HTTP web server

Static HTTP web server

# Python 3.x
python3 -m http.server 8000 --bind 127.0.0.1

# Python 2.x
python -m SimpleHTTPServer 8000
Shell One-linerspythonone-liner

Shell command: Static HTTP web server

💡 Use to check SSL/TLS config on moonlightanalytica.com before deploy

MoonlightJanusRobinhood
python: Static HTTP web server with SSL support

Static HTTP web server with SSL support

# Python 3.x
from http.server import HTTPServer, BaseHTTPRequestHandler
import ssl

httpd = HTTPServer(('localhost', 4443), BaseHTTPRequestHandler)

httpd.socket = ssl.wrap_socket (httpd.socket,
        keyfile="path/to/key.pem",
        certfile='path/to/cert.pem', server_side=True)

httpd.serve_forever()

# Python 2.x
import BaseHTTPServer, SimpleHTTPServer
import ssl

httpd = BaseHTTPServer.HTTPServer(('localhost', 4443),
        SimpleHTTPServer.SimpleHTTPRequestHandler)

httpd.socket = ssl.wrap_socket (httpd.socket,
        keyfile="path/tp/key.pem",
        certfile='path/to/cert.pem', server_side=True)

httpd.serve_forever()
Shell One-linerspythonone-liner

Shell command: Static HTTP web server with SSL support

💡 Audit your Moonlight site headers for security best practices

MoonlightJanusRobinhood

awk: Get the last hour of Apache logs

Get the last hour of Apache logs

awk '/'$(date -d "1 hours ago" "+%d\\/%b\\/%Y:%H:%M")'/,/'$(date "+%d\\/%b\\/%Y:%H:%M")'/ { print $0 }' \
/var/log/httpd/access_log

Shell command: Get the last hour of Apache logs

Remove a specific line from a file

Remove a range of lines from a file

sed: Replace newline(s) with a space

Replace newline(s) with a space

sed ':a;N;$!ba;s/\n/ /g' /path/to/file

# cross-platform compatible syntax:
sed -e ':a' -e 'N' -e '$!ba' -e 's/\n/ /g' /path/to/file
Shell One-linerssedone-liner

Shell command: Replace newline(s) with a space

💡 Analyze Janus server logs for error patterns

Janus
sed: Replace newline(s) with a space

Replace newline(s) with a space

# perl version (sed-like speed):
perl -p -e 's/\n/ /' /path/to/file

# bash version (slow):
while read line ; do printf "%s" "$line " ; done < file
Shell One-linerssedone-liner

Shell command: Replace newline(s) with a space

Delete string +N next lines

sed '/start/,+4d' /path/to/file

grep (7)

grep: Search for a "pattern" inside all files in the current directory

Search for a "pattern" inside all files in the current directory

grep -rn "pattern"
grep -RnisI "pattern" *
fgrep "pattern" * -R
Shell One-linersgrepone-liner

Shell command: Search for a "pattern" inside all files in the current directory

💡 Useful for Janus development and debugging workflows

Janus
grep: Show only for multiple patterns

Show only for multiple patterns

grep 'INFO*'\''WARN' filename
grep 'INFO\|WARN' filename
grep -e INFO -e WARN filename
grep -E '(INFO|WARN)' filename
egrep "INFO|WARN" filename
Shell One-linersgrepone-liner

Shell command: Show only for multiple patterns

💡 Add to your Janus development toolkit

Janus
grep: Except multiple patterns

Except multiple patterns

grep -vE '(error|critical|warning)' filename

Show data from file without comments

Delete first 10 lines (and backup original)

perl -i.orig -ne 'print unless 1 .. 10' filename

perl: Delete all but lines between foo and bar (and backup original)

Delete all but lines between foo and bar (and backup original)
